description_long: This is a dark wooden pagoda structure with three distinct, tiered roofs, each level slightly smaller than the one below, creating a classic stacked appearance. The roofs are hexagonal and feature pronounced overhangs, with the topmost tier capped by a pointed, ornamental finial. The main body of the pagoda is open-sided with wooden railings and balusters, allowing visibility and access from multiple directions. A set of stairs leads up to the main platform, which is elevated above the ground and surrounded by a low wooden fence. The wood appears polished and well-maintained, giving the structure a glossy, rich brown finish. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional East Asian park or garden pavilions, intended for shelter, rest, or ceremonial use.
description: A dark, polished wooden pagoda with three stacked, hexagonal roofs, open sides, and a decorative finial at the top. The structure features railings, stairs, and a glossy finish, evoking traditional East Asian architecture.
description_view_0: The pagoda is seen from an angle highlighting the stairs and open entrance, with all three roof tiers and the finial clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view centers the stairs and entrance, providing a direct look at the open front and the symmetry of the tiered roofs.
description_view_2: The pagoda is shown from a slightly offset angle, with the stairs visible to the left and the layered roofs casting deep shadows.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the pagoda, with the stairs and entrance directly aligned, emphasizing the central axis and the stacked roof design.
